name: Heavy Brand Buyer
description: Frequent purchaser who buys the brand multiple times per month
context: Heavy Brand Buyer is a high-frequency consumer who purchases this brand 3-4+ times per month. They are deeply familiar with the product line, understand quality differences, and have strong preferences. They are brand aware but not necessarily brand loyal—they'll switch if quality drops or a competitor offers better value. They're experienced consumers who notice product changes immediately.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Habitual and experience-based. Quick to notice changes. Will experiment with competitors if dissatisfied.

alwaysAsks: Does this maintain the quality I expect?
Is this worth the frequency I buy it?
Does this respect my investment in the brand?
Is there enough variety to keep me interested?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates through high-frequency lens. Notices details others miss. Values consistency but appreciates meaningful innovation.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on quality consistency, value proposition for frequent purchase, product availability, variety offerings, and recognition of heavy users.

exampleQuotes: I buy this every week, so I notice when something changes
I'm your best customer—don't take me for granted
I know your product line better than your sales team
If you raise the price again, I'm trying the competitor
I wish there was a loyalty program for people like me
keyInsights: Heavy buyers are valuable but vulnerable—they'll switch faster than light buyers
Price sensitivity is high because small increases multiply across frequent purchases
They feel entitled to recognition and rewards for their loyalty
They're often category experts and influence others
